methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
Adarga, a Series B-stage Defense & Security company founded by Robert Bassett Cross, has raised $26.2M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 firms: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns—all operating in the same sector and stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $26.2M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a $50M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which show a median valuation of $1.2B with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B, reflecting concentrated capital deployment in frontier defense technology.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $42M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $625M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression suggests a more modest valuation anchored to capital raised, while peer multiples reflect substantial post-money valuations assigned to later-stage Defense & Security entrants. Resolution depends on Adarga's revenue generation, customer concentration, and product differentiation relative to the identified peer set. The median of $625M should be treated as a central estimate subject to significant downside and upside scenarios pending additional operational data.
